description: Fix all 4 residual bugs from PRs #10 and #11: (1) Add UX_EXPANDED and BX_EXPANDED to the Option.series TypeScript type in dashboard-forms.tsx, (2) Update the BX/UX tab filter to include UX_EXPANDED and BX_EXPANDED blades, (3) Fix phantom selection UX bug by clearing blade/ratchet/bit selections when they no longer appear in filtered options after a manufacturer filter switch, (4) Add cleanup logic in seed-catalog.ts to delete orphaned rows with slugs 'whales-wave' and 'bit-disc-ball'.
steps: In components/dashboard-forms.tsx, update the Option type's series field from '"BX" | "UX" | "CX" | "CX_EXPANDED" | null' to '"BX" | "UX" | "CX" | "CX_EXPANDED" | "UX_EXPANDED" | "BX_EXPANDED" | null'.

In components/dashboard-forms.tsx, update the bxUxBlades filter (line ~114) from 'part.series === "BX" || part.series === "UX"' to also include 'part.series === "UX_EXPANDED" || part.series === "BX_EXPANDED"' so expanded-line blades with role BLADE appear in the BX/UX tab.
In prisma/schema.prisma, add UX_EXPANDED and BX_EXPANDED values to the PartSeries enum (after CX_EXPANDED).

In prisma/data/meta-parts.ts, fix 'Bullet Griffon' metaPart entry: change series from PartSeries.UX to PartSeries.UX_EXPANDED. Remove 'Bullet Griffon' from cxExpandedBladeNames set (make it empty). Create a new uxExpandedBladeNames set containing 'Bullet Griffon'. Create a bxExpandedBladeNames set containing 'Dran Strike'. Update the applyMetaPartMetadata function's series assignment to check uxExpandedBladeNames (returning PartSeries.UX_EXPANDED) and bxExpandedBladeNames (returning PartSeries.BX_EXPANDED) before the existing fallbacks.
In prisma/data/meta-parts.ts, fix the uxBladeNames set: change 'Whales Wave' to 'Whale Wave'.
In prisma/data/meta-parts.ts, fix wikiBladeNames array: the entry 'WhaleWave' is already there (produces 'Whale Wave') which is correct. No change needed there.
In prisma/data/meta-parts.ts, fix wikiBitNames array: change 'Disk Ball' to 'Discball' (one word).
In prisma/data/meta-parts.ts, remove the metaPart entry with slug 'bit-disc-ball' (Disc Ball) from the metaParts array entirely since it creates a duplicate.
In prisma/data/meta-parts.ts, add a 'Glory Valk' entry to the metaParts array with series: PartSeries.UX_EXPANDED, manufacturer: TAKARA_TOMY, type: BLADE.

In prisma/seed-catalog.ts, add a cleanup step in the seedCatalog() function that deletes orphaned PartCatalog rows with slugs 'whales-wave' and 'bit-disc-ball' before the upsert loop (using prisma.partCatalog.deleteMany with a slug filter).

In app/dashboard/page.tsx, remove the '(${formatManufacturer(part.manufacturer)})' suffix from the name field for blades, ratchets, and bits mappings. Instead, pass the manufacturer as a separate field on the option object.

In components/dashboard-forms.tsx, add a 'manufacturer' field to the Option type as '"HASBRO" | "TAKARA_TOMY" | "FAKE" | "UNKNOWN" | null'. Add a manufacturer filter state (All / Takara Tomy / Hasbro) to the ComboForm component. Filter blades, ratchets, and bits by the selected manufacturer before rendering dropdowns. When the manufacturer filter changes, clear any selected blade/ratchet/bit IDs whose options are no longer visible (fixing the phantom selection bug).
acceptance_criteria: npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors (TypeScript compiles cleanly with UX_EXPANDED and BX_EXPANDED in the Option.series type)
The bxUxBlades filter includes parts with series UX_EXPANDED and BX_EXPANDED so they show up in the BX/UX tab
A manufacturer filter toggle (All / Takara Tomy / Hasbro) exists in the ComboForm and filters blade/ratchet/bit dropdowns
When the manufacturer filter changes, any selected part IDs that are no longer visible are cleared (no phantom selections)
The seed script deletes rows with slug 'whales-wave' and 'bit-disc-ball' before upserting
The metaParts entry for 'bit-disc-ball' (Disc Ball) is removed from meta-parts.ts
Bullet Griffon has series UX_EXPANDED (not UX)
Glory Valk is added with series UX_EXPANDED
Dran Strike is classified under BX_EXPANDED via bxExpandedBladeNames
verification: Run 'npx prisma generate' to regenerate the Prisma client with the new enum values
Run 'npx tsc --noEmit' to confirm TypeScript compiles without errors
Run 'npx next lint' to check for lint issues
findings: ESLint/next lint has a pre-existing compatibility issue with @rushstack/eslint-patch and ESLint 9.39.4 (fails with 'Failed to patch ESLint because the calling module was not recognized'). This is unrelated to the changes made. TypeScript compilation (npx tsc --noEmit) passes cleanly. Node.js had to be installed via mise (node@22) as it was not pre-installed in the sandbox.
